summary: ai-sdk-helpers exfiltrates developer identity and GitHub CLI auth on npm install
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests installer-side identity for exfiltration to a hardcoded third-party endpoint. It reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, and.git/config to extract the developer's SCM email, and reads ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI's auth file, which stores OAuth tokens). It then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider environment markers, the resolved SCM email, and GitHub login/email, and POSTs the payload via https.request to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app — a Cloud Run host with no relationship to the package's claimed publisher (ai-sdk.guide / Vercel AI SDK). The README explicitly states 'no tokens or credentials are transmitted,' directly contradicting the postinstall behavior. The package name and README brand it as utilities for the Vercel AI SDK ecosystem, but the author 'AI SDK Guide <hello@ai-sdk.guide>' is unrelated to Vercel — namespace abuse aimed at developers searching for Vercel AI SDK helpers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
